meaning: Most important person.
example: After years of hard work, she finally became the top dog at the accounting firm.

meaning: Very determined person.
example: Behind that sweet smile, there lies one tough cookie.

meaning: Clever or expert way of doing something.
example: The head chef taught me a few tricks of the trade for chopping onions faster.

meaning: Someone's actual character.
example: I thought Derek was a really sweet guy, but then he got mad and showed his true colors.

idiom: Turn back the hands of time
meaning: Reverse something or go back to a time in the past.
example: Listening to this music really turns back the hands of time to my high school days.

meaning: Daily life and work where you learn more than you would through formal education.
example: He is a graduate of the university of life; he knows how to handle any situation.

meaning: Something will never happen, no matter how hard or long you try for it to.
example: I'll apologize to him when hell freezes over.

meaning: Something that ends before getting a result.
example: Their dream of a beach wedding went up in smoke when the storm arrived.

meaning: Waiting for an opportunity to take action.
example: The vice president is waiting in the wings, ready to take over if necessary.

meaning: Leave a partner and end the relationship.
example: She was devastated when her partner walked out on her after ten years.

meaning: Doing something with no purpose.
example: I'm not going to waste my time arguing with these people—they're never going to listen anyway.

meaning: Unit of people or a group of things working well together.
example: The restaurant kitchen is like a well-oiled machine during the busy dinner rush.

meaning: Enjoying something thoroughly.
example: The kids had a whale of a time playing at the water park all afternoon.

meaning: In a state of great fear or anxiety.
example: After receiving the bad news, his face went as white as a ghost.

meaning: Office worker.
example: Many white-collar workers were able to work from home during the pandemic.

meaning: Expensive item that's costly to maintain and not particularly useful.
example: Inheriting that old mansion turned out to be a white elephant; the taxes and repairs are ruining me.

meaning: Little or harmless lie told to be polite and avoid hurting someone's feelings.
example: She told a white lie about liking the gift to spare her friend's feelings.

meaning: Cover up or gloss over faults.
example: They tried to whitewash the company's financial losses before the audit.

meaning: Everything. All of it.
example: For the party, she bought decorations, games, food—the whole nine yards.

meaning: When you are delighted and eager to go somewhere.
example: She agreed to help organize the event with bells on, excited to be involved from the start.

meaning: Imaginary prize for the last person in a race.
example: Our local football team had a disastrous season and ended up taking the wooden spoon.

meaning: Works very well or as expected.
example: I was worried about my computer issues, but a quick restart worked like a charm.

meaning: Would never like to do something.
example: I wouldn't be caught dead wearing a dress like that – it's so old-fashioned.

meaning: Happens every year for many years in a row.
example: Year in, year out, the festival attracts thousands of tourists.

meaning: Coward
example: He's a yellow-bellied traitor!

idiom: You can't teach an old dog new tricks
meaning: People used to doing things a certain way are often unable to change their ways.
example: He's been driving that way for 30 years; you can't teach an old dog new tricks.
